mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive returns exceed 65% when FEDFUNDS > 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate negative repricing of equity valuations within five trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 55% of triggered instances or t-stat for return difference is < 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising retail sales and declining housing starts indicates unsustainable consumer leverage that inevitably reverses, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint signal shows no predictive power (partial R^2 < 0.01) or returns are positive in > 60% of divergence cases.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects robust manufacturing output that supports corporate profit growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days during expansion phases.
failure_condition: Signal flips to negative direction during expansion regimes or Rank IC drops below 0.03 in expansion windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained inflation above 202 on the CPI index forces aggressive monetary tightening expectations, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day returns are positive in > 60% of threshold-crossing events or correlation with forward returns is < -0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrates expanding economic capacity and consumer spending power, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC remains below 0.02 or positive return frequency drops below 50% during payroll growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: Synchronized expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based demand strength that translates into higher corporate earnings, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) or forward returns are negative in > 55% of synchronized expansion cases.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production signals weakening manufacturing demand that rapidly erodes equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns within five trading days during contraction phases.
failure_condition: Signal predicts positive returns during contraction regimes or 5-day return frequency exceeds 60% when INDPRO declines.
